mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS shows sustained positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and consequently S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances where UNRATE shows a significant increase.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) are low, it signifies an accommodative monetary environment. This reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat during periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are simultaneously low, failing to show a positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production (INDPRO) growth, manufacturing activity is strong, indicating healthy supply-side expansion. This positive economic momentum typically translates into short-term positive market sentiment and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO shows strong growth within a manufacturing expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market. This underpins strong consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns. This reduction in investment and consumer confidence negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by > 10% from its 6-month peak
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances where HOUST shows a significant decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising while GDP growth (GDPC1) is simultaneously slowing, it indicates a challenging economic environment where monetary policy is tightening into a weakening economy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in 3 months AND GDPC1 annualized growth rate decreases by > 1% over the last two quarters
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat in periods where FEDFUNDS is rising and GDPC1 growth is simultaneously decelerating, indicating no negative impact.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust GDP growth (GDPC1), market participants anticipate continued corporate profitability and overall economic expansion. This positive outlook drives short-term investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 shows strong growth during a robust economic expansion regime.
